ground_truth.title: Top 10 Most Desired Digital Cameras of 2025 Under $2500 | The Best Mirrorless Cameras on the Market
ground_truth.author: Myles Katherine
ground_truth.main_content:
You guys know I'm a Canon girlie through and through, but that doesn't mean I don't spend my days pining over other brands and wondering what it would be like. There are moments when I've considered selling all of my Canon gear and converting, just to try something new and inspire myself in an exciting way. Shooting with new gear is a great way to prevent burnout, especially when photography is your full-time job, like mine. It’s been 13 years since I first started my business and decided to commit to it fully. There have been moments where I've wanted to give up. Moments where the hustle was just too much. But whenever I start considering other professions, they just don't fulfill me in the way that photography does. Photography is my passion, my obsession and my true love. I'm about to head to the Bild Expo with B&H in New York City next week and I can't wait to learn and experience everything new that's going on in the photography industry. In the meantime, I wanted to share the cameras that have caught my eye this year and that I would recommend to photographers who want to enhance their photography game.

The Top 10 Most Desired Digital Cameras of 2025 Under $2500 include:

1. Canon EOS R6 Mark II - $2000. Features in-body stabilization, dual card slots, full-frame sensor, 4K video at 60 fps, 24.2 MP images, flip-out screen, and excellent low-light performance. Known for natural color tone and sharp images.

2. Sony A7 IV - $2500. 33 MP full-frame sensor, excellent video capabilities, 4K at 60 fps, in-body stabilization. Highly desired for videographers who dabble in photography.

3. Nikon Z6 Mark III - $2100. Capable of 6K video at 60 fps, partially stacked 24.5 MP sensor, faster autofocus. Known as a flagship camera with creative options like Rich Tone portrait mode.

4. Panasonic Lumix S5 II - ~$1700. 24 MP full-frame sensor, phase-detect AF, excellent in-body image stabilization, 6K video at 30 fps. One of the best IBIS systems available.

5. Fujifilm X-H2S - $2500. APS-C sensor with 6.2K video recording, fast autofocus, subject-tracking. Known for film-like quality and unique color experience with film-simulation modes.

6. Sony A6700 - $1400. 26 MP APS-C sensor, 4K video at 120 fps, compact size. Great for content creators at a lower price point with flip-out screen and image stabilization.

7. Canon EOS R8 - $1300. Budget version of R6 II with same full-frame sensor but no IBIS. Super lightweight and perfect for traveling.

8. Fujifilm X-S20 - ~$1200. Low-cost version of X-H2S with 6.2K video at 30 fps, 26.1 MP APS-C sensor, 5-axis IBIS. Great for content creators with dedicated Vlog mode.

9. Canon EOS RP - Budget-friendly full-frame mirrorless option

10. Leica SL2-S - Best Leica camera on a budget, offering exceptional quality for its price range.

By using affiliate links and codes in this blog post, it supports Myles Katherine Photography.
with: I can’t help but start off with this camera, because, yes, it's Canon. But I sit at home, hoping and praying that one of these will fall out of the sky and into my lap.
Sony is really good at almost getting me to convert. Their cameras have excellent video, superbly sharp images, incredibly full-frame sensors and super fast, reliable autofocus.
Oh, Nikon. They are pretty much Canon's main long-time rival and evil twin sister.
I'm not sure why this camera keeps calling my name, but I'm desperate to have one on my shelf at some point.
Ugh, I've ALWAYS wanted a Fujifilm camera! The Fujifilm X-H2S does not have a full-frame sensor but it still packs a powerful punch with it's APS-C sensor.
